Petition For An Order Determining The Sale Of Real Property Is Necessary To Pay Debts, Devisees, And Expenses Of Administration And Is To The Advantage Of The Estate And In The Best Interest F The Interested Persons
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for administrator Mohammed Irshad Monie.
Jeffrey Tsu for respondent Mohammed Rukshad Monie, who was also present.
Will Doyle, Guardian ad Litem for beneficiary Aishun Monie.

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Amended Petition for Order Determining Sale of Real Property is Necessary to Pay Debts, etc.; and 2) Third Report of Status of Administration and Petition for Extension of Time to Administer Estate.

Mr. Wrobel reported progress toward a framework for settlement, including issues relating to a creditor's lien and potential waiver, and anticipated resolution within approximately forty-five days.

Mr. Doyle reported that he had received the proposed settlement framework and was reviewing it, and that he was gathering information regarding the beneficiary's public benefits in order to assess the impact of any proposed distribution.

Following discussion, the Court took the third report on status of administration off calendar and set status hearings for close of estate and status of settlement for June 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

The Court continued the petition regarding sale of the real to June 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Off Calendar

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared via zoom for Mohammed Monie (petitioner).
Jeffrey Tsu appeared via zoom for and with Rukshad Monie (respondent).

Mr. Wrobel stated that they are on track to achieve a buyout, that the buyout would occur after final distribution.

Mr. Tsu stated that they need more time to raise money for a potential buyout of the property.

The matter is continued to February 11, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For An Order Determining The Sale Of Real Property Is Necessary To Pay Debts, Devisees, And Expenses Of Administration And Is To The Advantage Of The Estate And In The Best Interest F The Interested Persons
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Continued To Dec-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for administrator Mohammad Irshad Monie.
Jeffrey Tsu for respondent Mohammad Rukshad Monie, who was also present.
William Doyle, Guardian Ad Litem for beneficiary Aishun Monie.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Amended Petition for Order Determining Sale of Real Property is Necessary to Pay Debts, Devisees, and Expenses of Administration, etc.; and 2) Third Report on Status of Estate Administration and Petition for Extension of Time to Administer Estate.

Mr. Wrobel reported a change in circumstances which altered his client's position concerning a potential buyout, and that he and Mr. Tsu were discussing framework for resolving the matter. He requested a 45-day continuance to allow time to finalize terms.

Mr. Tsu agreed but stated that his client needed additional time to gather funds. He did not object to Mr. Wrobel's request for 45 days.

Mr. Doyle informed the Court that he had reviewed the case but had not yet participated in settlement discussions. The Court directed counsel to include Mr. Doyle in all further communications.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 3, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For An Order Determining The Sale Of Real Property Is Necessary To Pay Debts, Devisees, And Expenses Of Administration And Is To The Advantage Of The Estate And In The Best Interest F The Interested Persons
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Continued To Dec-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order Determining The Sale Of Real Property Is Necessary To Pay Debts, Devisees, And Expenses Of Administration And Is To The Advantage Of The Estate And In The Best Interest F The Interested Persons
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Continued To Oct-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for administrator Mohammed Irshad Monie (via Zoom).
Razeefa Monie, interested party, in attendance (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on Mohammed Irshad Monie's Amended Petition for Order Determining Sale of Real Property is Necessary to Pay Debts, etc.

The Court asked for a status update. Mr. Wrobel reported that no binding offer had been accepted. He Stated that the most recent offer contained errors and was too low, and that his clients were prepared to offer significantly more to buy out the opposing interest.

The Court noted that an unverified objection had been submitted, with verification filed after the deadline had passed. The Court declined to default the objecting party.

The Court indicated that it intended to appoint guardian ad litem for the decedent's sister Aishun Monie, pursuant to an ex parte request previously submitted.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to October 13, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing. The Personal Representative Shall File Either A Petition For Order For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration Pursuant To Prob. Code Sec.12200 Et Seq. Two Weeks Before The Hearing Date.

Petition For An Order Determining The Sale Of Real Property Is Necessary To Pay Debts, Devisees, And Expenses Of Administration And Is To The Advantage Of The Estate And In The Best Interest F The Interested Persons
Off Calendar Jun-30-2025 Continued To Sep-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for Mohammed Irshad Monie, Petitioner/Administrator (via Zoom).
Jeffrey Tsu appeared via zoom for and with Mohammed Rukshad Monie, Respondent (in person) .

Discussion was held regarding the mediation, that the petitioner wanted to sell the house, that respondent believes the brother wants him out of the house, and that the property is in very poor condition.

Mr. Tsu stated that his client would like to close out the estate and get his share of the money to buy back the property. Rukshad Monie stated that the current appraisal on was conducted last year.

The Court directs all parties to meet and confer and if not able to meet a resolution, Mr. Tsu to file a competing petition.

Status on mediation is ordered off calendar.

Amended Petition for Order Determining Sale of Real Property etc., is continued to September 8, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Filing of the Verfied Objections to the Amended Petiton is due by August 18, 2025. The Court will deem objections waived if not filed by the deadline.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

Thomas Wrobel, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Mohammed Irshad Monie, Petitioner.

Jeff Tsu, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Mohammed Rashad Monie, in court, Objector.

At the court's query, counsel offer an update regarding the status of the Estate specifically, the Estate's Real Property. After additional discussion, including the court's suggestion that the parties attempt mediation, the Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Counsel is instructed to monitor the court's website to obtain a copy of the order. Mediation must be completed no later than Mar-19-2025. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Mar-28-2025. The subject matter is continued to Apr-07-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to provide a status of mediation on that day.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for Mohammad Irshad Monie (petitioner).
Jeffrey Tsu for and with Mohammed Rukshad Monie.

Mr. Tsu stated that they don't want the property to be sold, that his client has lived there all of his life, that there is a disabled child of the decedent who does not understand what she is signing, and that he would like an order that confirms one third of the property to each sibling and they can then work out a deal. Mr. Tsu also stated that his client is concerned about his brother evicting him. Mr. Wrobel stated that there is no need for limited authority, that there is no plan to evict, and that he has secured a bond for his client under a controlled agreement. Mr. Wrobel also stated that the sister is not under a conservatorship and has legal capacity. The Court stated that going forward it will need to take steps to protect the sister's interest. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the court's intent to grant with limited authority.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To Mar-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for Mohammed Irshad Monie.
Mohammed Rukshad Monie in propria persona.

Attorney Jeffrey Tsu checked in on zoom, but was unable to remain on as he was not feeling well. Mr. Wrobel stated that he spoke with Mr. Tsu, that Mr. Tsu stated that he did not want to delay the opening of probate, and that he was fine with the petition being granted with limited authority. Rukshad stated that he wants to buy his brother out. Discussion was held regarding the restrictions of having limited authority. Mr. Wrobel stated that he is contemplating petitioning to the court for approval of a settlement agreement. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 15, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Mochammed Rukshad Monie (Decedent's Son/Objector) appeared in-person. Thomas Wrobel, Esq. appeared via Zoom for Mohammed Irshad Monie (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the matter was on calendar for a petition to open probate and that this matter was to be granted. Mohammed Rukshad Monie stated that he was objecting to the petition, that he needs time to retain a lawyer, that the petitioner is his brother, that his brother is an ex-felon, that they have a sister that is not included in this, that he had previously spoken with attorney who told him to come back after this hearing, and that the attorney recommended that an independent (Debra Dolch) be appointed. Mr. Wrobel stated that Ushan Monie (the Sister) is listed in the petition, that a bond of $900,000 has been arranged for his client, that his client is not in jail or in prison now, that his client is nominated by his sister, and that he would be happy to speak with Mohammed Rukshad Monie after this hearing. The Court briefly addressed Mochammed Rukshad Monie stating that the fact that his brother is bondable is significant.

At the conclusion of comments/argument, the Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Written objections are due by February 15, 2023.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: H. Singh. Matter not reported.
